title: Day 21 — Nowruz Under Fire: IDF Strikes Tehran on Persian New Year; Iran's 66th Wave (True Promise 4); Qatar Halts All LNG Output; Brent Hits $115
detail: Day 21 of Operation Epic Fury / Roaring Lion — coinciding with Nowruz, the Persian New Year. Israel pounded Tehran with airstrikes Friday morning local time as Iranians marked Nowruz, with the IDF writing on social media that it had 'begun a wave of strikes targeting infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran.' The attacks came a day after Israel pledged to refrain from more strikes on South Pars gas field. Iran responded with Operation True Promise 4's 66th wave, deploying five super-heavy and multi-warhead missiles targeting Tel Aviv and US military bases in the region. UAE reported 7 Iranian ballistic missiles and 15 drones intercepted in a single barrage. In a major secondary escalation, Qatar was forced to halt all gas production after Iranian counterattacks on energy infrastructure — Qatar is one of the world's biggest LNG producers, and the shutdown disrupts global supplies and fertilizer production. Brent crude hit $115/bbl Thursday morning before retreating on Asian market open Friday. The Pentagon submitted a $200 billion supplemental war funding request to the White House — described by Trump as 'a small price to pay.' The UAE announced it has disrupted a Hezbollah-Iran terrorist network arrested for money laundering and financing terrorism under fictitious commercial cover. Europe and Japan issued a joint statement prepared to join 'appropriate efforts' for safe Hormuz passage (UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan). Iran rejected all ceasefire talks: FM Araghchi stated 'the United States started it and is responsible for all the consequences.' IDF claims 70%+ of Iran's missile launchers and 85%+ of detection and defense capabilities neutralized. CENTCOM: 7,000+ total targets struck since Feb 28. Iran's missile launch rate has fallen 90%+ from Day 1 peak (~350/day → ~25/day).

title: IDF Strikes Tehran on Nowruz — 'Targeting Infrastructure of the Iranian Terror Regime'
detail: Israel launched a new wave of airstrikes against Tehran on Nowruz, the Persian New Year — a psychologically calculated moment. The Israel Defense Forces wrote on social media: 'We have begun a wave of strikes targeting infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran.' The attacks came a day after Israel pledged to refrain from further strikes on the South Pars gas field, but resumed against capital infrastructure targets. Tehran residents described hearing multiple explosions across the city, with security forces banning Nowruz gatherings and paramilitary officials roaming largely deserted streets. The strikes signal that despite Day 19–20 diplomatic signals of possible restraint, the campaign against Iran's regime infrastructure is continuing. Netanyahu earlier this week had urged Iranians to 'go out and celebrate' Nowruz, saying IDF aircraft were 'enabling the brave people of Iran' to mark the holiday — a calculated psychological information operation.

title: Iran Launches 66th Wave — Operation True Promise 4 (Super-Heavy Multi-Warhead Missiles)
detail: Iran carried out the 66th wave of Operation True Promise 4, deploying five super-heavy and multi-warhead missiles. Multiple alerts across West Asia and the Gulf indicated fresh drone and missile activity. According to the IRGC's Public Relations Office, the operation was executed 'successfully', striking targets 'in the heart and south of the occupied territories' — Tel Aviv and central Israel — along with US military bases in the region. UAE air defenses intercepted 7 Iranian ballistic missiles and 15 drones in a single Thursday barrage, with the UAE Ministry of Defence releasing video of interceptions. Analysts note that despite the 90%+ reduction in daily launch rates from Day 1 peaks, Iran is qualitatively escalating with heavier, more sophisticated warhead types targeting populated areas. The Israeli population has been repeatedly driven to bomb shelters across the country.

title: Qatar Forced to Halt All LNG Production Following Iranian Counterattacks on Energy Infrastructure
detail: Qatar has been forced to halt all gas production — a cascading consequence of Iranian counterattacks on energy infrastructure across the Gulf following Israel's March 19 strike on South Pars gas field. Qatar is one of the world's biggest producers of liquefied natural gas (LNG) and the disruption is reverberating globally, disrupting supplies of fuel and essential fertilizers used in food production. The UN World Food Programme and economic analysts have warned the 2026 escalation in Iran is driving significant long-term increases in global food prices. Qatar's North Field (the world's largest natural gas reservoir, continuous with Iran's South Pars) is at the center of the disruption. The halt represents the most dramatic single energy production disruption since the conflict began — exceeding even the Kharg Island oil terminal strike in near-term economic impact.

title: Pentagon Seeks $200 Billion Supplemental War Funding — Trump Calls It 'Small Price to Pay'
detail: The Pentagon has submitted an extraordinary $200 billion supplemental funding request to the White House for the Iran war. President Trump confirmed the request, calling it 'a small price to pay' to equip the military. Republican Senator Susan Collins, chair of the Senate Appropriations Committee, told reporters she had not yet been notified — suggesting the request bypassed normal congressional notification protocols. The $200 billion request is on top of extra DoD funding already received in Trump's budget reconciliation bill. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed US forces remain 'on plan' to achieve their objectives but declined to specify when the war might end. The scale of the funding request is unprecedented for a single military campaign in US history and signals the administration anticipates extended operations. Congressional Democrats have twice forced unsuccessful war powers resolution votes since the conflict began.

title: UAE Dismantles Hezbollah-Iran Terrorist Financial Network — Operatives Arrested
detail: The United Arab Emirates announced it has disrupted a terrorist network funded and operated by Lebanon's Hezbollah and Iran, arresting all its operatives. According to UAE state news agency (WAM), the network was involved in 'money laundering, financing terrorism and threatening national security,' and had been 'operating within the country under a fictitious commercial cover,' seeking to infiltrate the UAE national economy and carry out 'external schemes threatening the country's financial stability.' No immediate comment from Hezbollah or Iran. The UAE's action — while under active Iranian missile attack — signals that Gulf states are simultaneously sustaining active military defense while also conducting domestic counter-terrorism operations against Iranian and Hezbollah-linked financial networks. Multiple Iranian and Hezbollah networks had established commercial fronts in Dubai's financial ecosystem over decades.

title: UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan Prepare to Join Safe Hormuz Passage Efforts
detail: The United Kingdom, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, and Japan issued a joint statement prepared to join 'appropriate efforts' to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, and condemned Iranian attacks on shipping and energy infrastructure. The EU also called on all parties for a moratorium on striking critical infrastructure, as growing concerns mount about the Iran war's impact on the global economy. The EU's 27 leaders collectively called for de-escalation. The Hormuz strait remains effectively closed to US, Israeli, and Western-allied vessels under Iran's selective blockade — though Iran has allowed China- and India-flagged vessels to transit. The international coalition for maritime safety represents the broadest multilateral response to the strait closure since the conflict began, though it falls short of NATO Article 5 trigger or a military escort mission.

title: Iran Rejects All Ceasefire Scenarios — FM Araghchi: 'No Scenario for a Ceasefire'
detail: Iran has doubled down on its rejection of cease-fire talks on Nowruz. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared: 'The United States started it and is responsible for all the consequences of this war, human and financial, whether for Iran, for the region, or for the entire world.' A senior Iranian security official stated that 'at this stage there is no scenario for a ceasefire and no relevant option for ending the war.' Iran also asserted that Nowruz represented an opportunity to 'steel resolve,' not negotiate. This directly contradicts Trump administration messaging suggesting the war would end 'in the very near future.' Iran's rejection of negotiations came as the Iranian Red Crescent reported more than 18,000 civilians injured and tens of thousands of civilian structures damaged. The conflict has killed more than 2,300 people across Iran, Lebanon, and Israel. US State Department authorized emergency arms sales to Gulf countries to bolster air defense capabilities.
